optimizations: Simplify the design by reducing the number of faces and features ($20-$30 savings)
Use a more efficient machining strategy, such as using a ball nose endmill instead of a square endmill ($40-$50 savings)
risks: High machining cost due to complex geometry (high face count, high surface area)
Potential for tool wear or breakage due to 5-axis machining of steel
Setup cost may be underestimated for a part with such complexity
